# mapbox_token (Resource)

Manages a Mapbox access token. Tokens provide API access to Mapbox services with specific scopes and optional URL restrictions.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "mapbox_token" "example" {
  note   = "My API token"
  scopes = ["styles:read", "fonts:read"]

  allowed_urls = [
    "https://example.com",
  ]
}

output "token_id" {
  value = mapbox_token.example.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `scopes` (Set of String) The set of scopes granted to the token (e.g., `styles:read`, `fonts:read`).

### Optional

- `allowed_urls` (List of String) A list of URLs that the token is restricted to. Maximum 100 URLs.
- `note` (String) A human-readable description of the token.

### Read-Only

- `created` (String) The ISO 8601 timestamp when the token was created.
- `default` (Boolean) Whether this is the default token for the account.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the token.
- `modified` (String) The ISO 8601 timestamp when the token was last modified.
- `token` (String, Sensitive) The actual token string. This value is sensitive and will not be displayed in logs.
- `usage` (String) The token type: `pk` (public) or `sk` (secret).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import mapbox_token.example <token_id>
```
